title: Realty Income Prices Debut €1.1 Billion Dual-tranche Offering of Euro-denominated Senior Unsecured Notes
text: SAN DIEGO , June 29, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Realty Income Corporation (Realty Income, NYSE: O), The Monthly Dividend Company®, today announced the pricing of a public offering of €550 million of 4.875% senior unsecured notes due July 6, 2030 (the "2030 notes"), and €550 million of 5.125% senior unsecured notes due July 6, 2034 (the "2034 notes"). The public offering price for the 2030 Notes was 99.421% of the principal amount for an effective annual yield to maturity of 4.975%, and the public offering price for the 2034 Notes was 99.506% of the principal amount for an effective annual yield to maturity of 5.185%.

title: Realty Income Creates More Value Per Unit Capital Than NNN
text: Realty Income and NNN REIT are both spread investors in the retail REIT sector, generating rental income on assets they own and profiting from the difference between rent received and the cost of obtaining the assets. Realty Income has a lower cost of capital, allowing them to create more value than NNN REIT, with a cap rate-WACC difference of 0.75% compared to NNN REIT's 0.35%. Realty Income offers investors a larger slice of the value pie, with a total return of 9.74% compared to NNN REIT's 8.45%, making it the better deal at the current price.

title: Realty Income: Still A Better Choice Than Fixed Income For Retirees?
